domains.name: Domain 2: Data Mining, Analysis, and Visualization
Domain 3: Data Governance, Quality, and Controls

questions.text: During peak traffic, the platform team must distinguish structured and unstructured data. What should they do FIRST?

options.text: Cable types (not recommended for production workloads)

questions.text: While troubleshooting latency, engineers determine they must describe data life cycle stages. Which action is appropriate?

questions.text: A company needs to define primary keys in datasets. Which option meets the requirement with the LEAST implementation effort?

Two designs were proposed. Design A would distinguish structured and unstructured data. Design B would describe data life cycle stages. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Data type drives tooling. Compare with Collection, processing, analysis, storage, sharing: Life cycle informs controls.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would describe data life cycle stages. Design B would compare relational and dimensional models. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Life cycle informs controls. Compare with Star/snowflake schemas for analytics: Models affect query performance.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would compare relational and dimensional models. Design B would define primary keys in datasets. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Models affect query performance. Compare with Unique identifiers per entity grain: Keys enforce relational integrity.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would define primary keys in datasets. Design B would distinguish structured and unstructured data. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Keys enforce relational integrity. Compare with Tables vs documents/media: Data type drives tooling.

questions.explanation: Governance reduces data risk. Compare with Catalog showing sources and transforms: Lineage supports audits.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would document data lineage. Design B would define data retention schedules. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Lineage supports audits. Compare with Policy-based retention and deletion: Retention limits risk and cost.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would define data retention schedules. Design B would assign data stewards. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Retention limits risk and cost. Compare with Named owners per dataset: Stewards accountability improves quality.

questions.text: Two designs were proposed. Design A would assign data stewards. Design B would measure data quality dimensions. The team must pick the approach aligned with vendor best practices. Which choice is correct?
questions.explanation: Stewards accountability improves quality. Compare with Accuracy, completeness, timeliness metrics: Quality metrics guide remediation.
